title: Idempotency, Inbox Transactions, and Effects · Independent Go Trial
summary: An independent Go integration team receives different topology, traffic, schema, tenant, dependency, and shutdown constraints and transfers the module into a new a duplicate-safe Go effect state machine. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a duplicate-safe Go effect state machine with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive a stable scoped operation identity from producer intent, tenant, message type, and semantic version rather than delivery metadata; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Record inbox identity, input fingerprint, state transition, domain effect, and completion atomically in one database transaction where possible; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Reject reuse of an operation identity with a different payload fingerprint or semantic intent and retain conflict evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Stable Idempotency Key
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go messaging team handles case 1660 during Go publisher supervisor losing one state notification. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject fingerprint conflicts, suppress completed duplicates, and preserve external ambiguity; separate deterministic browser proof from race-enabled and live-broker evidence; then add a subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Stable Idempotency Key.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for derive a stable scoped operation identity from producer intent, tenant, message type, and semantic version rather than delivery metadata; in go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence. For RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Stable Idempotency Key, keep Stable Idempotency Key connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Audit the initial evidence packet · Lab Inbox Retention Replay
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go messaging team handles case 3279 during stream-versus-queue architecture board. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject fingerprint conflicts, suppress completed duplicates, and preserve external ambiguity; reject stale connection and channel generations; then introduce a keyed sequence gap during single-active failover.
